title: Fixes in Imagenet training script
body:
* ngpus_per_node variable count fix
* dist_backend assertion added for nccl issue

step_plan.summary: Fixes the ImageNet training script by correctly deriving `ngpus_per_node` from the actual CUDA device count and by adding a fail-fast validation so `--dist-backend nccl` is only allowed when CUDA GPUs are available, preventing NCCL initialization hangs/crashes.

steps.operation: Fix ngpus_per_node computation and add a fail-fast guard for multiprocessing-distributed on GPU-less nodes
steps.reason: The number of processes spawned via `mp.spawn(..., nprocs=ngpus_per_node)` and the world-size scaling depend on `ngpus_per_node` being the true CUDA device count. Using `torch.cuda.is_available()` (or similar) can incorrectly yield `1` even when there are zero visible GPUs (misconfigured CUDA, `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` masking, etc.), causing incorrect spawn counts and rank/world-size math. When `--multiprocessing-distributed` is requested but `torch.cuda.device_count() == 0`, the script should error clearly rather than spawning invalid workers.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior from silently spawning 1 process to raising a clear error on misconfigured GPU environments. Impacts distributed launch paths that rely on `--multiprocessing-distributed`; ensure single-process CPU training paths remain unaffected. Update/verify any docs or example commands expecting implicit fallback.

steps.operation: Add an assertion/validation that NCCL backend is only used when CUDA GPUs are available (and the per-process GPU selection is valid)
steps.reason: `dist.init_process_group(backend='nccl', ...)` requires CUDA-capable devices; using NCCL on CPU-only environments (or when no GPUs are visible/selected) can hang or crash during initialization. A precondition check immediately before initializing the process group provides a deterministic, user-friendly failure message and guides users toward `gloo` for CPU training.
steps.side_effects: Users who previously invoked `--dist-backend nccl` on CPU (or with no visible GPUs) will now fail fast with a clear message instead of encountering NCCL initialization issues. Might require adjusting CI or example invocations that accidentally set `nccl` without GPUs.
